title: GE Aerospace Announces AerCap Selects GEnx Engines to Power 15 Additional Boeing 787 Dreamliners
author: Paul Bergman
description: AerCap Holdings, the world's largest owner of Boeing 787 aircraft, has selected GE Aerospace's GEnx-1B engine to power an additional 15 Boeing 787 Dreamliners. The GEnx engine has demonstrated a 99.98% dispatch reliability rate and stays on-wing at three times the rate of competing engines. GE Aerospace is investing over €110 million in European facilities and $1 billion across U.S. manufacturing sites to increase production capacity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong order confirmation from major customer AerCap for 15 additional aircraft engines, demonstrating market confidence. Engine shows superior reliability metrics (99.98% dispatch rate) and significant operational achievements (50 million flight hours). Company is making substantial capital investments ($1+ billion) to expand production capacity, indicating strong demand and growth trajectory.

Continued investment in proven engine technology for fleet expansion demonstrates confidence in business model and customer demand. Selection of GEnx engines for additional 15 aircraft shows strategic commitment to reliable, economical aircraft platforms that support customer requirements.
Indirect positive sentiment as major aircraft lessor AerCap continues to order Boeing 787 Dreamliners equipped with GEnx engines, indicating sustained demand for Boeing's widebody aircraft platform.
Indirect positive sentiment as major aircraft lessor AerCap continues to order Boeing 787 Dreamliners equipped with GEnx engines, indicating sustained demand for Boeing's widebody aircraft platform.

title: SpaceX vs. Archer Aviation: Which Aerospace Stock Is a High Flyer for 2026?
author: Brendan Coffey
description: SpaceX and Archer Aviation represent different bets on aerospace innovation. SpaceX has proven revenue scale ($18.7B in FY2025) with Starlink and reusable rockets, though it reported a $5B net loss. Archer Aviation is pre-commercial with only $300K revenue but lower debt and potential military/cargo pathways. For 2026, SpaceX is recommended as the better investment due to its established business model and path to profitability, while Archer faces significant regulatory hurdles and won't turn profitable until 2030.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Established market leader with $18.7B revenue (33% YoY growth), proven Starlink business providing revenue diversification, strong market support evidenced by $85.7B IPO, and analyst projections showing path to profitability by 2027. Despite negative free cash flow, the company has real operational scale and competitive advantages.

Early-stage company with minimal revenue ($300K) and significant losses ($618.2M), but lower debt-to-equity ratio (0.1x) and potential regulatory tailwinds. Faces substantial certification risks, high P/S valuation (1,590x), and won't achieve profitability until 2030. Represents speculative opportunity with considerable execution risk.

Early-stage company with minimal revenue ($300K) and significant losses ($618.2M), but lower debt-to-equity ratio (0.1x) and potential regulatory tailwinds. Faces substantial certification risks, high P/S valuation (1,590x), and won't achieve profitability until 2030. Represents speculative opportunity with considerable execution risk.

Mentioned as a strategic partner providing conditional purchase agreement for up to $1B in Archer aircraft, indicating industry validation but representing contingent future revenue dependent on Archer's certification success.
Mentioned as competitive threat to SpaceX in aerospace market, representing established industry competition but not directly analyzed in the article.

title: Griffin Global Asset Management Announces the Delivery of the First of Six Boeing 737 MAX 8 Aircraft on Long-Term Lease to VietJet Group
author: Na
description: Griffin Global Asset Management has delivered the first of six Boeing 737 MAX 8 aircraft on long-term lease to VietJet Group. The deal supports VietJet's fleet modernization and expansion strategy with fuel-efficient aircraft.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The 737 MAX 8 aircraft selection by VietJet represents continued market demand for Boeing's aircraft and supports recovery in commercial aviation orders.
The 737 MAX 8 aircraft selection by VietJet represents continued market demand for Boeing's aircraft and supports recovery in commercial aviation orders.

title: GEnx-1B Engines Surpass 50 Million Flight Hours
author: Na
description: GE Aerospace announced that its GEnx-1B engine has reached 50 million flight hours in just over 14 years, the fastest rate for any GE commercial widebody engine. The engine powers Boeing 787 Dreamliners and maintains a 99.98% dispatch reliability rate. GE Aerospace is investing $1 billion in U.S. manufacturing and supply chain enhancements to meet growing demand.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Boeing 787 Dreamliner continues to benefit from the highly reliable GEnx-1B engine with 99.98% dispatch reliability and strong adoption by over 50 operators worldwide, supporting the aircraft's market competitiveness and operational success.

Boeing 787 Dreamliner continues to benefit from the highly reliable GEnx-1B engine with 99.98% dispatch reliability and strong adoption by over 50 operators worldwide, supporting the aircraft's market competitiveness and operational success.

Company achieved a significant operational milestone with the GEnx-1B engine reaching 50 million flight hours faster than any previous model, demonstrating strong market adoption and reliability. Additionally, the $1 billion investment in manufacturing and supply chain indicates confidence in future growth and capacity expansion.

title: Archer Aviation vs. AST SpaceMobile: Which Aerospace Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Brendan Coffey
description: The article compares two aerospace technology stocks: Archer Aviation, developing electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for urban air mobility, and AST SpaceMobile, building a space-based cellular broadband network. While Archer faces regulatory certification hurdles and slower revenue generation, AST SpaceMobile shows stronger revenue growth trajectory and competitive moats through partnerships with major telecom operators. The analyst recommends AST SpaceMobile as the better buy for 2026 due to its faster path to profitability and established partnerships, despite higher capital expenditures.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company shows promise with regulatory framework development and major partnerships (United Airlines, U.S. Air Force, Stellantis), but faces significant challenges including minimal revenue ($300K in FY2025), substantial losses ($618.2M), high cash burn ($511.7M negative FCF), and long runway to profitability (projected 2030). Described as 'highly speculative' and 'wait-and-see' stock.

Company shows promise with regulatory framework development and major partnerships (United Airlines, U.S. Air Force, Stellantis), but faces significant challenges including minimal revenue ($300K in FY2025), substantial losses ($618.2M), high cash burn ($511.7M negative FCF), and long runway to profitability (projected 2030). Described as 'highly speculative' and 'wait-and-see' stock.

Company demonstrates strong revenue growth ($70.9M in FY2025 vs $4.4M prior year), strategic partnerships with major telecom operators (AT&T, Verizon, Vodafone), high competitive moat, and faster path to profitability (projected 2027). Expected to reach positive free cash flow by 2029. Analyst explicitly recommends this as the better buy for 2026.
